name: CDKTF (CDK for Terraform)

auth.notes: Uses Terraform provider credentials (AWS, GCP, etc.)

agent_readiness.notes: Write Terraform in TypeScript/Python/Java/C#/Go. Synthesizes to Terraform JSON (cdktf synth), then runs terraform plan/apply. Languages: TypeScript (best support), Python, Java, Go, C#. Provider bindings generated from Terraform providers via cdktf get. Stack abstraction. TerraformStack, TerraformResource. All 1000+ Terraform providers accessible. cdktf deploy / cdktf diff. Benefits: type safety, loops, conditions, abstractions in real code. Steeper learning curve than HCL for simple infra. Pulumi is alternative with better multi-cloud story.

gotchas: Still pre-1.0 in some languages — API stability risk
Synthesizes to JSON Terraform — debugging can be tricky
Provider bindings can be huge (AWS provider = 300MB+)
best_when: Teams with existing Terraform state wanting TypeScript/Python abstractions
Complex infra needing loops, conditionals, and OOP impossible in HCL
Organizations standardizing on Terraform but wanting type-safe IaC
Reusable infrastructure module libraries in TypeScript
avoid_when: Greenfield IaC (Pulumi may offer better DX)
Teams not already using Terraform ecosystem
summary: CDKTF lets you write Terraform in TypeScript, Python, Java, Go, or C#. Synthesizes to Terraform JSON for plan/apply. Access all 1000+ Terraform providers with type safety. Loops, conditionals, OOP abstractions impossible in HCL. Best for teams with existing Terraform state wanting to bring programming language power to infra definitions. Pulumi offers cleaner DX for greenfield IaC without Terraform dependency.
